title: 2026 Brady List (Walla Walla County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Walla Walla County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Your Walla Walla County public records request #26-9 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
Walla Walla County ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-9 has been submitted successfully. This letter serves as Walla Walla County’s initial five-day letter, pursuant to RCW 42.56.520, to your public records request. After your request is reviewed for content and we determine the department or office in which your request refers to, the County will provide an estimate to provide an initial installment of responsive records. After locating responsive records, the County will determine whether any of the responsive documents are subject to applicable exemptions under the Public Records Act, and to notify any third parties affected by the request. If third party notification is required, twenty (20) business days will be allowed for a response by the third party to those records which specifically pertain to them. As records are reviewed, they may be released on an installment basis. Additionally, the Washington Public Records Act (RCW 42.56.120) and Walla Walla County Resolution 23-201 now allow the County to charge for provision of electronic records. Refer to the hyperlinked documents for the fee schedule for future records installments. The due date for your request may be later than usual because the office is closed on: * January 19: Martin Luther King, Jr. Day * February 16: Presidents' Day ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact Walla Walla County.

After searching for responsive records, we have found several files responsive to this request. An email with directions on how to download responsive records will be/has been sent separately. An exemption log, if applicable, is provided with responsive records with appropriate exemptions for redactions to affected documents. Please contact Walla Walla County within thirty (30) days if you have additional questions, or if you believe identifiable and responsive records have not been provided. This is Walla Walla County’s final, definitive response to this records request. Walla Walla County considers this request closed. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.550, you have one year from today to seek judicial review of Walla Walla County’s response to this records request. Any action brought more than one year from today may be dismissed outright by a court as untimely.  ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? title: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Kent Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Kent Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

## PRESERVATION DEMAND ##

Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt.

Please immediately:

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval.

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution.

## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ##

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation.  Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ##

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production.

## FEE REQUEST ##

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

## RECORDS REQUESTED ##

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals.

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced.

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request:

	Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles
	(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered
	but vehicles are believed to remain in service):
	c. 50816D  — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010)
	d. 50887D  — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031)
	e. 54180D  — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031)
	f. 43500D  — Bomb squad trailer (2005)
	g. 26073D  — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997)
	h. 41461D  — Communications van (2005)
	i. 44372D  — Command vehicle (2005)
	j. 54148D  — Riot fencing trailer (2011)
	k. 54362D  — CSI van with generator (2012)
	l. 50295D  — SWAT cutaway van (2008)
	m. 54117D  — CSI van (2011)
	n. 24208D  — Weapons vehicle (1987)
	
	Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern):
	o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP"
	p. All plates with the prefix "XMT"
	
	Other:
	a. BCH-478
	b. 826-ZVA

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

public records. The City estimates  it will provide a further response
within 5 business days with a determination regarding disclosure, denial, or if
an exemption to disclosure can be established. The City reserves the right to
extend this time frame if necessary.

I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request 

## RECORDS REQUESTED ## 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals. 

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced.

03/19/2026 Re: Public Records Request, dated 3/12/2026 File #W078473-031226 for Other Public Records Matter – Extension of Time Needed to Respond Dear Phil  Mocek: 
On 3/12/2026, you submitted a public records request to the City of Kent wherein you sought: To Whom It May Concern: 

## PRESERVATION DEMAND ## 

Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt. 

Please immediately: 

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval. 

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution. 

## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ## 

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation. Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf. 

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ## 

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production. 

## FEE REQUEST ## 

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request 

## RECORDS REQUESTED ## 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals. 

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced. 

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request:

(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Phil Mocek

The City now needs  to extend this estimate; and currently estimates it will be able to provide responsive records, either on an installment basis or in total by 04/02/2026. 
This additional time is necessary due to current staffing levels, current volume of public record requests received; locating and assembling the requested records; if necessary notifying any third parties who may be affected by the request; and to determine whether any of the information contained within the requested records is exempt from disclosure, in whole or in part. If you have any questions, please contact the City Clerk’s Office at 253-856-5725. Thank you. Sincerely, Tiffany Alcorn Tiffany Alcorn CPRO - Lead Public Disclosure Administrator City of Kent Administration

Hi Tiffany and Pat.

I'll bring a USB drive so I can copy what I need without requiring anything from your staff--no printing, no emailing, no fee.

Since inspection is free under RCW 42.56.120(1), the $90.50 copying fee from your April 1 letter won't apply to what I inspect in person. And since no appointment is needed under RCW 42.56.080, I'm just flagging this in advance so the records can be pulled and ready before I arrive rather than having anyone scramble when I show up.

Thanks for your help with this.

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #W078473-031226
communications.communication:
RE: Public Records Request — ALPR Scan Records; Fee and Format Dispute

Dear City of Kent Public Records Officer,

This responds to the City's April 1, 2026 correspondence. The requester disputes the fee methodology applied to both installments, objects to the production format, and renews the fee waiver request the City's April 1 letter did not address.

## I. The Fee Methodology Must Reflect Actual Cost ##

RCW 42.56.120(2)(b)(iii) authorizes a fee of $0.05 per four electronic files "uploaded to an email, cloud storage service, or other electronic delivery system." This fee is calculated per file uploaded — not per row of data within a file, and not per image associated with a database record. The validity of the City's fee calculation therefore depends entirely on how many discrete files are actually uploaded, which depends on the production format addressed in Section II.

The requester requests that the City provide in writing: (1) whether it has conducted a cost study pursuant to RCW 42.56.120(2)(b) or is applying the statutory default schedule; (2) if applying the statutory default, the published declaration required by WAC 44-14-07001 stating why calculating actual costs would be unduly burdensome; and (3) identification of the specific policy or mayoral directive authorizing the fee schedule applied. Web research has not identified any such published declaration by the City of Kent. Without it, the City lacks the required legal predicate for applying the statutory default rate, and the fee demand is accordingly unsupported. The requester requests production of that declaration if it exists.

## II. Producing a Structured Database as 500,000 Discrete Image Files Is Inconsistent with the City's Obligations Under RCW 42.56.100 and RCW 42.56.120 ##

The request sought all ALPR scan records and associated metadata, and explicitly requested production in electronic database format, including CSV, PDF, or JSON export. ALPR scan records are generated and stored as structured data within the Flock Safety platform — a cloud-based database system. Each scan record is a database entry comprising structured metadata fields: timestamp, GPS coordinates, plate number, camera identifier, confidence score, and associated image reference. The records do not exist natively as 500,000 discrete, independent image files; they exist as a structured dataset, the native export format of which is a single tabular data file such as a CSV.

Flock Safety provides authorized agency administrators a bulk export function as a standard platform feature. Producing a single CSV export of the responsive dataset requires no custom programming, no specialized IT expertise, and no per-file upload process. The actual cost to the City of transmitting a single CSV file is de minimis.

RCW 42.56.100 requires agencies to provide "the fullest assistance" to requesters. RCW 42.56.120 requires that "[w]hen calculating any fees authorized under this section, an agency shall use the most reasonable cost-efficient method available to the agency as part of its normal operations." The native bulk CSV export available through the Flock Safety administrative interface is both more reasonable and more cost-efficient than extracting 500,000 individual image files. Recharacterizing a structured database as 500,000 discrete files to trigger a per-file fee — where a single-file export is available, was explicitly requested, and requires no custom programming — is not consistent with either provision.

The requester requests that the City produce the responsive records as a single native database export (CSV, JSON, or equivalent) from the Flock Safety system and recalculate any applicable fee on that basis. Under this methodology, the fee for installment #2 would be $0.05 for one file, not $6,250.00 for 500,000 files.

## III. The Fee Waiver Request Has Not Been Addressed ##

The original request expressly requested fee waiver, citing research and public interest purposes, the nonprofit nature of the MuckRock platform through which the request was submitted, and the minimal actual cost of electronic production under RCW 42.56.120. The City's April 1 letter does not address this request. The requester renews it and requests a written response, including the specific basis for any denial.

## IV. Notice of Intent to Inspect ##

Pursuant to RCW 42.56.080, the requester provides notice of intent to inspect the records identified as responsive to installment #1 in person at the City Clerk's Office, 220 4th Avenue South, Kent, WA 98032, during published business hours (Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.). In-person inspection carries no charge under RCW 42.56.120(1); the $90.50 fee stated in the April 1 letter does not apply to records inspected in person. The requester will bring a USB drive for self-copying; no agency copying fee will be incurred for records copied using the requester's own equipment. This notice is provided so that the records may be made ready before arrival. This notice also stops the running of any abandonment period under the City's records policy pending resolution of the fee and format dispute regarding installment #2.

## V. Requested Actions ##

The requester requests that the City:

1. Provide in writing: (a) whether it has conducted a cost study under RCW 42.56.120(2)(b) or is applying the statutory default; (b) if the latter, the published unduly-burdensome declaration required by WAC 44-14-07001; and (c) the specific policy or mayoral directive authorizing the fee schedule applied;
2. Produce installment #2 as a single native database export (CSV, JSON, or equivalent) from the Flock Safety system and recalculate the applicable fee accordingly; and
3. Provide a written response to the fee waiver request, including the basis for any denial.

The requester requests a response within five business days.

Cordially,
Phil Mocek

communications.subject: Other Public Records Updated :: W078473-031226
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
05/08/2026 Phil Mocek MuckRock News, DEPT MR206498 Boston, MA 02115 Re: Public Records Request, dated 3/12/2026 File #W078473-031226 for Other Public Records Matter – Installment # 1 - Records Available; Installment #2- Additional Time Needed to Respond Dear Phil Mocek:

"On 3/12/2026, you submitted a public records request to the City of Kent wherein you sought records related to automated license plate readers in the City of Kent. On 4/1/2026, the City indicated that the first installment of records was available and that copies could be obtained upon the City’s receipt of your fee payment.

 

As you may be aware, on 3/30/2026, Engrossed Substitute Senate Bill 6002 (“SB 6002”) was signed into law and became effective that same day. Section 5(5) of this bill provides that “[a]utomated license plate reader data is not subject to disclosure under the public records act, chapter 42.56 RCW … ” Automated license plate reader (“ALPR”) data is defined in Section 2(3) of this bill as “all data collected by automated license plate reader systems including, but not limited to, global positioning system coordinates, location, date and time, speed of travel, photograph, license plate number, automobile characteristics, or other identifying information.”

 

At the time of the City’s 4/1/2026 communication to you, the City had believed that the above referenced sections of SB 6002 were inapplicable to public records requests that had been received by a public agency before this new legislation took effect. However, in the time that has passed since, the City Attorney’s Office has advised that, consistent with Serv. Emps. Int'l Union Local 925 v. Dep't of Early Learning, 194 Wn.2d 546 (Wash. 2019) and Wash. Educ. Ass'n v. Dep't of Ret. Sys., 21 Wn. App. 2d 736 (Wash. Ct. App. 2022), those records that meet the definition of “ALPR data” are no longer subject to disclosure and must be withheld consistent with SB 6002, even for those public records requests that were received by an agency prior to that law taking effect. Under Washington State case law, certain newly created exemptions under the Public Records Act are to be assessed at the time the records are made available for public inspection or copying, as opposed to the date the public records request was originally made. As a result, those records you requested that meet the definition of “ALPR data” under SB 6002 are no longer able to be inspected or released to you in response to your public records request.

 

The City of Kent has completed an updated Installment #1 of records relating to the above-referenced public records request. As noted below, this Installment #1 is being provided consistent with SB 6002 and any records it may exempt from disclosure or that may now require additional redaction.

 

As indicated above, ALPR data is not subject to disclosure pursuant to Section 5(5) of SB 6002. The City will therefore be withholding records that include ALPR photos, identifying data collected by ALPR systems, and other records included in the statutory definition of “ALPR data.” The records that are being withheld as part of this Installment #1 are noted in the accompanying exemption log. An additional log will accompany Installment #2 where exempt records are withheld.

 

Some records are being redacted in accordance with the Public Records Act and may include redactions where an exempt portion of the record was “blacked out” or a record withheld in its entirety. The records are provided to you along with an exemption log that identifies the specific exemption authorizing the withholding/redacting, and a brief explanation of how the exemption applies to the record withheld, in whole or in part.

 

There is no applicable fee for the records being provided under Installment #1 due to the size of the files. While we believe your inquiries regarding the City’s fee structure sent on 5/1 and 5/3 are now moot, the information you requested can be found and accessed at this link.

 

The records included within Installment #1 are available at the City’s on-line Public Records Center.  You may access the records by following the below link and instructions:

1. Visit KentWA.gov/PRR

2. Click on "My Request Center"

3. Click on "View My Requests"

4. Login to your account, click "Submit"

5. Click on the File # for this request.

6. The electronic records are available in the "Files" window.


In accordance with the City's Public Records Request Policy located on the City's website; copied records, or records gathered for inspection, will be available to the requester for 30 calendar days from the date of the City's notification to the requester that the records are available.  If a requester fails to retrieve or inspect the responsive records within that 30-day period, the records request will be deemed abandoned, the records will be returned to the originating department, and the City will close the request. If the records were produced as an installment response under section 1.5.3.4.C, the requester's failure to retrieve or inspect an installment will result in the City discontinuing its search for any remaining records, closing the entire records request.

 

You had previously indicated that you desired to inspect these records in person to avoid fees. While this updated installment does not have any applicable fees, please contact our office at 253-865-5725 to schedule an appointment for your inspection if you would still wish to inspect records in person. Due to cybersecurity concerns, we cannot allow requesters to interface with City devices via their own USB devices. We are happy to discuss other methods of providing copies of records prior to any scheduled inspection.

 

We are continuing to process responsive records for Installment #2. The City currently estimates it will be able to provide responsive records, either on an installment basis or in total by 05/21/2026. 


If you are not able to access the records at the Public Records Center, or if you have any questions, please contact the City Clerk’s Office at 253-856-5725.

 

Thank you.

Sincerely,

Tiffany Alcorn

Tiffany Alcorn

CPRO - Lead Public Disclosure Administrator


City of Kent Administration
Arthur “Pat” Fitzpatrick, Chief Administrative Officer"

communications.subject: Other Public Records Updated :: W078473-031226
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
05/20/2026 Re: Public Records Request, dated 3/12/2026 File #W078473-031226 for Other Public Records Matter – Full Response Completed, File Closed Dear Phil  Mocek: On 3/12/2026, you submitted a public records request to the City of Kent wherein you sought Other Public Records. On 05/08/2026 you were provided with installment #1. As of 05/20/2026 the City has made available the records responsive to your request. In producing these records, the City made a reasonable search to locate responsive records based on the description you provided in your request. The fee to obtain copies of the responsive records has been waived. Some records are being withheld in accordance with the Public Records Act and may include redactions where an exempt portion of the record was “blacked out” or a record withheld in its entirety. The records are provided to you along with an exemption log that identifies the specific exemption authorizing the withholding/redacting, and a brief explanation of how the exemption applies to the record withheld, in whole or in part. The records are available at the City’s on-line Public Records Center.  You may access the records by following the below link and instructions: 1. Visit KentWA.gov/PRR 2. Click on "My Request Center" 3. Click on "View My Requests" 4. Login to your account, click "Submit" 5. Click on the File # for this request. 6. The electronic records are available in the "Files" window. The Public Records Act one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run and the City does not intend to further address this request. To seek internal review of any denials of requests, please refer to the City of Kent Public Records Policy 1.5 located at Kentwa.gov/prr. This letter is to confirm completion of your request and to advise the City is closing its file. If you have any questions regarding the closing of this request, or believe responsive records have not been provided, please contact the City Clerk within 30 calendar days at 253-856-5725. Thank you. Sincerely, Tiffany Alcorn Tiffany Alcorn CPRO - Lead Public Disclosure Administrator Office of the City Clerk Kim Komoto, City Clerk 220 Fourth Avenue South Kent, WA 98032 253-856-5725

title: NY police data collection request (Utica Police Department)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: NY police data collection request (Utica Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. The total number of use of force incidents, separated by year used from 2016-2021 (for example, information that specifies the total number of use of force incidents in 2016, total number of use of force incidents in 2017, etc.).
2. The total number of use of force incidents, separated by type of force and year used from 2016-2021 (for example, information that specifies the number of taser incidents in 2016, baton incidents in 2017, etc.).
3. the total number of civilian complaints alleging any form of law enforcement misconduct that were reported and the total number sustained, separated by year from 2016-2021 (for example, 8 complaints in 2020, 2 complaints sustained in 2020, 4 complaints in 2021, 1 complaint sustained in 2021, etc.)
4. the total number of civilian complaints alleging law enforcement use of excessive force that were reported and the total number sustained from 2016-2021, separated by year
5. the total number of civilian complaints alleging biased policing or racial profiling that were reported and the total number sustained from 2016-2021, separated by year
6. the total number of officer involved shootings incidents from 2016-2021, separated by year (excluding shootings of animals and accidental discharges that are not directed at a person)

When available, I would appreciate being sent individualized data for each item in this request in spreadsheet format. If individualized data are unavailable, please send information that includes aggregate statistics for the item instead.

Note that my requests for civilian complaints are specifically for civilian complaints alleging police misconduct, please do not include complaints filed by one officer/the department against another officer.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please see our open records request for our client Jemeel Frederick regarding date od loss 04/25/2026.

I have attached a copy of the police report for your reference. If you need additional information in order to process our request, please let me know. Thank you.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessica Gerstner

title: May 12, 2026 executive session minutes — Bid 22515 Simes House

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: May 12, 2026 executive session minutes — Bid 22515 Simes House
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 30A §22(g)(2), I respectfully request:

1. The minutes of any executive session of the Plymouth Select Board held on or about May 12, 2026 in which Bid 22515 (sale of the Joseph Simes House) was discussed.
2. The Select Board's most recent review under M.G.L. c. 30A §22(g)(1) of whether those minutes can now be released.
3. The specific exemption claimed under M.G.L. c. 30A §21(a) for that executive session, including the purpose number and the published rationale.

If any portion remains exempt at this time, please produce the non-exempt portions and calendar the remainder for periodic review per §22(g)(1). Per M.G.L. c. 66 §10, please respond within 10 business days.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

communications.subject: Request# 2026-0879 : Request Submission Acceptance Confirmation
communications.communication:
Please DO NOT reply to this email !! Please use the email address of the sender at bottom of this email, in order to communicate with the request management team. 
Town of Plymouth, MA 
Public Record Request Number:2026-0879 
Requester: Edward Havey 
Request Date: Wednesday, May 20, 2026 7:30:00 AM 
Response Due Date: Thursday, June 4, 2026 

Dear  Edward Havey : 

We have received your public Records request for Town of Plymouth. This record is being assigned to appropriate department. The department reviewer will review the request and create an effort estimate to create the response. Per state mandate, for Town of Plymouth, if the effort estimate is more than 2 hours, we will create a proposed cost estimate and will email such estimate to your attention. Once you approve the amount and send the payment, we will start working on your request. 

In case the estimate is less than 2 hours, we will proceed to work on the response and will send you the response once the work is completed. Please also note that state mandate allows us to complete and send the response within 10 business days. We will do our due diligence towards this effort. However, the due date may be extended for any of the following reasons: 
1.   Any clarification that is required from you towards this request will stop the clock. The clock will be resumed once the proper clarification is received. 
2.   Payment of estimated effort – The time it takes to receive the due amount will be added to the original due date. 
3.    If in the opinion of the reviewer it is determined that original due date cannot be met despite reasonable effort, the due date shall be extended based on reviewer’s judgment. You will be promptly notified in such case with revised due date. 
Thank you. 
Connor Anderson 
Records Access Officer 
Town of Plymouth 
26 Court St. 
Plymouth, MA 02360 
Tel: (508)-322-3388 x10213 
Email: canderson@plymouth-ma.gov

communications.subject: Request# 2026-0879 : Response to your Request
communications.communication:
Please DO NOT reply to this email !! Please use the email address of the sender at bottom of this email, in order to communicate with the request management team. Town of Plymouth, MA Public Record Request
Number:2026-0879 
Requester: Edward Havey 
Request Date: Wednesday, May 20, 2026 7:30:00 AM 
Response Due Date: Thursday, June 4, 2026 
Dear
Edward Havey: 
We have completed the work in reference to your
request as referenced above. The response is given
below. Request Response 1. The Select Board Executive Session minutes from 5/12/2026 have not been released at this time. 
2. The Select Board has not yet reviewed whether or not the 5/12/2026 Executive Session Minutes can be released at this time. 
3. The information you are requesting is located on the 5/12/2026 Select Board Agenda. All meeting minutes and agendas are posted on the Town's website here: https://plymouth-ma.gov/agendacenter Attachments 
This
response may or may not contain separate Response Documents to include specific
response and data. In case such separate response documents exist, they are
represented by attachments to this response email. Therefore, please look for any
attachments if they exist. Between Request Response and attachments, we believe
you are receiving a comprehensive response to your request. 
If you have
any questions or comments, please do not hesitate to contact us at the following
email address. 
Thank you. Connor Anderson 
Records Access Officer 
Town of Plymouth 
26 Court St. 
Plymouth, MA 02360 
Tel: (508)-322-3388 x10213 
Email: canderson@plymouth-ma.gov 
If you object to the Town’s response, in accordance with G.L. c. 66, § 10 and 950 CMR 32.00, you may appeal to the following office: Supervisor of Public Records, Office of the Secretary of the Commonwealth, Public Records Division, One Ashburton Place, 17th Floor, Boston, MA 02108. Under the Public Records Regulations, all appeals to the Supervisor must include a copy of the original request, any response by the RAO and a statement indicating the reason for the appeal. The requester must also provide a copy of the appeal petition to the RAO. By law, the Supervisor is required to respond within 10 business days of receipt of your appeal. In accordance with G.L. c. 66, § 10A(c), you have the right to seek judicial review of an unfavorable decision by commencing a civil action in the Superior Court.

title: White Pine County Nevada Community & Economic Development Department

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: White Pine County Nevada Community & Economic Development Department
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- A record of public records requests submitted to the department that lists the date of request, the requester's name, the requester's organizational affiliation, a description of the request and the department's response for the years 2016, 2017 and 2018.

- Records of policies/procedures relating to the processing of public records requests, for the years 2016, 2017 and 2018.

- Records of total direct revenue and expenses (i.e., not including overhead) relating to the processing of public records requests in 2016, 2017 and 2018.

- Current schedule of fees relating to the processing of public records requests.

For clarification of this request, please contact me.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Richard Karpel

communications.communication:
Hello, I have attached PRRs from 2016-2018. The policies and procedures for PRRs are found in Nevada Revised Statutes.  Here is a link to that: https://www.leg.state.nv.us/nrs/nrs-239.html . My office does not segregate financial records for PRRs. We have changed receipting systems since then and the records are in storage. If you want 3 years of all of our financial records for every single financial transaction we completed, let me know. The fee for any PRR is $.50 per name per year and $.50 per page for copies of filed documents.  Let me know if you need anything else. Thank you

title: City of Patterson: Photographs of graffiti uploaded to the City of Patterson website

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: City of Patterson: Photographs of graffiti uploaded to the City of Patterson website
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All photographs of graffiti uploaded via the city of Patterson's website. The city of Patterson has an online form for reporting graffiti located at:

https://www.pattersonca.gov/FormCenter/Code-Enforcement-11/Reporting-Graffiti-78

This is a request for just the photographs. This is not a request for any other identifying information. I am explicitly not asking for identifying information from the form itself. 

Since the photos are of items in public view, there is a diminished expectation of privacy for the "subject" of the photo.

Time frame for request: January 1st 2023 to March 1st 2026. 

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. There is a public interest in seeing what has been flagged as graffiti by the public to the city of Patterson. 

There is a real and legitimate public interest in understanding the prevalence of graffiti in Patterson, what's reported to the city as graffiti, and in understanding the efficacy of Patterson's graffiti abatement efforts. 

Thank you to the city of Patterson for the city's commitment to transparency, and I look forward to working together to resolving this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Franklin Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Franklin Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by the chair of the select board related to their elected position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on personal and secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: RE: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Franklin Clerk)
communications.communication:
Good morning,

Thank you for your Right-to-Know request dated March 19, 2026 submitted pursuant to RSA 91-A, seeking records related to:

All email communications sent and received by the chair of the select board related to their elected position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on personal and secondary accounts.
The City has begun coordinating with the appropriate department(s) to identify responsive records. Based on our preliminary review, the estimated volume of responsive records is approximately 170 electronic communications.

Estimated Processing Time

Due to the volume of records and the time required to collect, review, and redact any information that may be exempt from disclosure under RSA 91-A, the City estimates the request may take approximately:

5 business days, estimated availability of records on April 17, 2026
(Monday through Friday, excluding state and federal holidays)

This estimate reflects the scope of the request and the need for staff to complete the work alongside normal departmental responsibilities.

Estimated Fees

Based on the current estimate, the following fees may apply under the City’s fee schedule:

Paper copies (if requested)

  *   $1.00 first page, $0.25 each additional page

Electronic communications (emails/messages)

  *   $1.00 for first 251 communications, $1.00 each additional thereafter

USB drive

  *   City supplied USB $10.00
  *   Self-provided USB, must be new, in sealed original packaging, no charge

In-Person Inspetion

  *   No charge, by appointment only

Fee Notes


  *   The electronic communication fee applies regardless of whether records are inspected, printed, or provided electronically.
  *   If records are provided electronically, they may be placed on a City-supplied USB drive ($10) or a new USB drive provided by the requestor in sealed packaging.

Record Access Options


  *   In-person inspection at City Hall by appointment only
  *   Pickup of a USB drive by appointment only
  *   Mail delivery if a prepaid shipping label is provided

Payment of Fees

Payment may be made by cash or check payable to the City of Franklin.

If the scope of the request or estimated cost changes during processing, the City will notify you before proceeding.

Narrowing the Request

If you wish to narrow or modify the scope of your request, doing so may significantly reduce both the processing time and the number of records produced. The City would be happy to work with you to clarify the request if helpful.
Next Steps
Please let us know if you would like to move forward with this request and which fulfillment method you prefer. All fulfillment methods, except mailing, are available by appointment only.
Paper Copies

  *   $1.00 for the first page
  *   $0.25 for each additional page
Electronic Communications (emails/messages)

  *   First 250 communications: no charge
  *   $1.00 per communication beyond the first 250
  *   Records will be provided on a USB drive
USB Drive Options

  *   City-provided USB: $10.00
  *   Requestor-provided USB: no charge
     *   Must be new and in sealed original packaging
In-Person Inspection

  *   No charge
If you are unable to pick up the records, please send a prepaid shipping label and let us know whether you would like printed copies or a USB.
If we do not hear from you within two weeks, the City will consider this request administratively closed. You are welcome to submit a new request at any time.
Sincerely,



[cid:image001.png@01DCCB26.D76839C0]

Brenda Demers
Executive Secretary | City Manager’s Office

City of Franklin
316 Central St
Franklin, NH 03235

(603) 934-3900


 The information contained in this e-mail is confidential and may also be privileged information. The information is intended for the use of the individual or entity to whom it is addressed.  If you are not the intended recipient, or an employee or agent responsible to deliver this information to the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any use, distribution, or copying of this communication is strictly prohibited. title: Open Records Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Flock Raven (El Monte Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Raven (El Monte Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Flock Raven records created by the department and Flock Safety
Databases, spreadsheets, and any other documents that provide the following information collectively or individually:
- Number of alerts
- Description, details about the alert (Examples: gunfire, car crash, distress, sideshow)
- List of alert types (Examples: gunfire, car crash, etc)
- Maps and alert locations
- Raven locations
- Response times
- False positives, false negatives, and any record about the technology's accuracy
- Audo recordings of alerts
- Access logs, reason for searches or alerts, audits

Also search documents:
Promotional materials, user guides, and any PowerPoint presentations related to Flock Raven
Contracts and MOU's related to Flock Raven

Policies around Raven and other audio detection technologies
List of agencies your department shares access with and agencies sharing their Raven's with your department 

Please search January 2023 to today's date.
Release files and documents in their original formats in a rolling production.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

title: MLB correspondence with Florida Department of Law Enforcement (FDLE) | Felipe Vázquez

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: MLB correspondence with Florida Department of Law Enforcement (FDLE) | Felipe Vázquez
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) All phone, text or email correspondence between your department and Major League Baseball (MLB) staff referencing Felipe Vázquez (DOB: 7/5/1991) between the following range:

09/1/2019-08/17/2021

While correspondence between your department and MLB staffers may include conversations beyond the following names, I've provided these people to assist with your search:

Moira Weinberg:
moira.weinberg@mlb.com
Rick Burnham:
rick.burnham@mlb.com
Mehtab Brar:
mehtab.brar@mlb.com
Liam Rich:
liam.rich@mlb.com
Erika Cook:
erika.cook@mlb.com

2) All records referencing Felipe Vázquez (DOB: 7/5/1991), including:

-- 911 calls (including call logs, audio and transcripts)
-- arrest reports
-- investigative records (which can include chronological records of events, property and evidence logs, witness lists, interview notes from officers examining the case, photos and videos included in the case file, search warrant affidavits, case summaries, and charges recommended to the prosecuting agency)

between the following ranges:
09/1/2019-08/17/2021

3) We're also requesting a FOIA log for related requests referencing Felipe Vázquez (DOB: 7/5/1991). We want:

1) The log or file number
2) The date the request was received
3) The name of the persons/organization (besides ourselves) making requests related to the above
4) A description of the information sought
5) The date the response was sent
6) The type of response your office sent (granted, denied or partial release)

If you determine that some but not all the information is exempt from disclosure and that you intend to withhold it, I ask that you redact it for the time being and make the rest available on a rolling basis. In any event, please provide a signed notification citing the legal authorities on which you rely if you determine that any information is exempt and will not be disclosed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge and processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I also ask that our request be filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available (email: holler@bwd.nyc and yelenadzhanova@gmail.com) – if not, secure file transfer, thumb drive, or CD-ROM. I am also available via phone at 718.360.6359 to discuss any questions you have about the request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Bradford Davis and Yelena Dzhanova

communications.subject: FDLE Office of General Counsel
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting the Florida Department of Law Enforcement Office of General Counsel. This message is being provided as confirmation your message was received. You should receive a response to your inquiry as soon as possible.


If you need criminal history records, please visit our website, https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/Criminal-History-Records/Obtaining-Criminal-History-Information.aspx, and/or contact FDLE’s Criminal History Services Section at ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us> or (850) 410-8161.

Public records requests are processed in the order that they are received. Processing time may be lengthened due to the nature and extent of the request. Searches may be negative and FDLE may not have records responsive to your public records request. You may also receive an invoice for your request depending on the nature and extent of your public records request. Please feel free to contact us at (850) 410-7676, or publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us, if you have additional specific information relating to the records you are requesting, as this may help expedite your request and/or reduce costs that may be associated with the request.

Additional information regarding how to obtain public records including what costs may be applicable is contained from the hyperlink below. We are committed to our fundamental values of Service, Integrity, Respect, and Quality.

For Additional Information click http://www.fdle.state.fl.us/OGC/Public-Records.aspx<https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/OGC/Public-Records.aspx>

communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Davis and Ms. Dzhanova:

RE:      Public Records Request Confirmation Receipt, FDLE Docket No. PRR-2023-2850
Requesting public records related to Felipe Vázquez.

This email serves to confirm receipt of your public records request. If you need criminal history records, please visit our website, http://www.fdle.state.fl.us/Criminal-History-Records/Obtaining-Criminal-History-Information.aspx, and/or contact FDLE’s Criminal History Services Section at ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us> or (850) 410-8161.

Public records requests are processed in the order that they are received. Processing time may be lengthy due to the nature and extent of the request. Searches may be negative and FDLE may not have records responsive to your public records request. You may also receive an invoice for your request depending on the nature and extent of your public records request. A copy of the FDLE Guide to Public Records is attached for your review.

Please be advised that FDLE may not be the custodian of all of the records you requested. FDLE is not the custodian of all State of Florida criminal incident-related records for every state and local criminal justice agency.

Please feel free to contact us if you have additional specific information relating to the records you are requesting, as this may help expedite your request and/or reduce costs that may be associated with the request.

Additional information regarding how to obtain public records including what costs may be applicable may be found on our website http://www.fdle.state.fl.us/OGC/Public-Records.aspx.
We are committed to our fundamental values of Service, Integrity, Respect, and Quality.

If you have questions concerning your request, please contact us at (850) 410-7676 or publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us>, and please include your “PRR” docket number we’ve provided above.

Sincerely,

Office of General Counsel
Florida Department of Law Enforcement
Post Office Box 1489
Tallahassee, FL 32302-1489

Please feel free to contact us if you have additional specific information relating to the records you are requesting, as this may help expedite your request and/or reduce costs that may be associated with the request. If you no longer need these records please let our office know.

Public records requests are processed in the order that they are received. Processing time may be lengthy due to the nature and extent of the request. Searches may be negative and FDLE may not have records responsive to your public records request. You may also receive an invoice for your request depending on the nature and extent of your public records request. A copy of the FDLE Guide to Public Records is attached for your review.

Please be advised that FDLE may not be the custodian of all of the records you requested. FDLE is not the custodian of all State of Florida criminal incident-related records for every state and local criminal justice agency.

Additional information regarding how to obtain public records including what costs may be applicable may be found on our website http://www.fdle.state.fl.us/OGC/Public-Records.aspx.
We are committed to our fundamental values of Service, Integrity, Respect, and Quality.

If you have questions concerning your request, please contact us at (850) 410-7676 or publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us>, and please include your “PRR” docket number we’ve provided above.

Sincerely,

Wanda

Office of General Counsel
Florida Department of Law Enforcement
Post Office Box 1489
Tallahassee, FL 32302-1489

communications.communication:
Dear Bradford Davis and Yelena Dzhanova:

RE: Public Records Request Response, FDLE Docket No. PRR-2023-3284

Thank you for contacting the Florida Department of Law Enforcement Public Records Section. We are working diligently to process your request. Processing time may be lengthy due to the number of public records requests pending fulfillment ahead of your request and due to the nature and extent of all processing public records requests.

If you no longer need these records please let our office know.

If you have further questions concerning your request, please contact us at (850) 410-7676 or publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us>, and please include your “PRR” docket number we’ve provided above.

Sincerely,

Office of Open Government Public Records Section

communications.subject: Confirmation of Public Records Request
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting the Florida Department of Law Enforcement Office of Open Government. This message is being provided as confirmation your message was received. You should receive a response to your inquiry as soon as possible.



If you need criminal history records, please visit our website, https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/Criminal-History-Records/Obtaining-Criminal-History-Information.aspx, and/or contact FDLE’s Criminal History Services Section at ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us> or (850) 410-8161.



Public records requests are processed in the order that they are received. Processing time may be lengthened due to the nature and extent of the request. Searches may be negative and FDLE may not have records responsive to your public records request. You may also receive an invoice for your request depending on the nature and extent of your public records request. Please feel free to contact us at (850) 410-7676, or publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us>, if you have additional specific information relating to the records you are requesting, as this may help expedite your request and/or reduce costs that may be associated with the request.



Additional information regarding how to obtain public records including what costs may be applicable is contained from the hyperlink below. We are committed to our fundamental values of Service, Integrity, Respect, and Quality.



For Additional Information click here: Open Government (state.fl.us)<https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/Open-Government>

communications.subject: FW: Public Records Request Response, FDLE Docket No. PRR-2023-2850
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Davis and Ms. Dzhanova,

RE:      Public Records Request Response, FDLE Docket No. PRR-2023-2850
Requesting public records related to Felipe Vázquez
Good-faith Deposit Invoice, $121.07

NOTE: Partial responsive records are included with this good-faith deposit invoice.

Please regard this as a good-faith deposit invoice for $121.07 for research, retrieval & review of your public records request.  Based on the information you provided in your request, this will require extensive use of information technology resources, extensive clerical and/or extensive supervisory assistance. Costs are assessed pursuant to s. 119.07(4)(a) and s. 119.07(4)(d), Florida Statutes (http://www.leg.state.fl.us/Statutes). This may not be the final amount due, but the best estimate of initial costs available at this time.

Labor Costs Already Expended Processing Request
Salary Rate
Position Title
Hours Expended
Labor Total
$21.63
OOG GOC II
0.50
$10.82 waived
TOTAL BALANCE DUE FOR COSTS ALREADY INCURRED
$10.82 waived
Estimated Initial Labor Costs for Request
Salary Rate
Position Title
Est. Hours
Est. Labor Total
$21.63
OOG GOC II
1.50
$32.45
$19.40
FMROC EA I
0.50
$9.70
$19.23
OOG GOC I
4.00
$76.92
Estimated Duplication & Postage Costs for Request
1 Disk
$2.00
TOTAL GOOD-FAITH DEPOSIT DUE
$121.07

A “policy of requiring an advance deposit seems prudent given the legislature’s determination that taxpayers should not shoulder the entire expense of responding to an extensive request for public records.” “[T]he reasonableness of a policy and its application — based on the facts in a particular case — guides whether an abuse of discretion is shown.” Morris Publishing Group, LLC v. State, 154 So.3d 528, 534 (Fla. 1st DCA 2015).

A copy of the arrest warrant is being provided with this good-faith deposit invoice. This record is provided pursuant to Florida’s Public Records Law, Chapter 119, Florida Statutes. Portions exempt from public disclosure pursuant to the following have been redacted.

s. 119.071(5)(a)5., F.S. Social security numbers are confidential and exempt from s. 119.07(1) and s. 24(a), Art. I of the State Constitution;

s. 119.0712(2)(b), F.S. Personal information contained in a motor vehicle record;

18 U.S. Code § 2721(a)(1) Driver’s Privacy Protection Act of 1994 (DPPA): Prohibition on release and use of certain personal information from State motor vehicle records personal information, as defined in 18 U.S.C. 2725(3), about any individual obtained by the department in connection with a motor vehicle record;

s. 119.071(2)(h)1.b., F.S. Sexual offense victim information is confidential and exempt from s. 119.07(1) and s. 24(a), Art. I of the State Constitution;

s. 119.071(2)(j)1., F.S. Victim of sexual battery, aggravated child abuse, aggravated stalking, harassment, aggravated battery, or domestic violence is exempt from s. 119.07<http://www.leg.state.fl.us/Statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&Search_String=&URL=0100-0199/0119/Sections/0119.07.html>(1) and s. 24(a), Art. I of the State Constitution;

s. 827.071 (5)(a), F.S. It is unlawful for any person to knowingly possess, control, or intentionally view a photograph, motion picture, exhibition, show, representation, image, data, computer depiction, or other presentation which, in whole or in part, he or she knows to include any sexual conduct by a child. The possession, control, or intentional viewing of each such photograph, motion picture, exhibition, show, image, data, computer depiction, representation, or presentation is a separate offense. If such photograph, motion picture, exhibition, show, representation, image, data, computer depiction, or other presentation includes sexual conduct by more than one child, then each such child in each such photograph, motion picture, exhibition, show, representation, image, data, computer depiction, or other presentation that is knowingly possessed, controlled, or intentionally viewed is a separate offense. A person who violates this subsection commits a felony of the third degree, punishable as provided in s. 775.082, s. 775.083, or s. 775.084; and

Marsy’s Law, Section 16(b)(5) of Article I of the Florida Constitution.

After our office receives your good-faith deposit payment your public records request will return to the queue of records requests for processing. You may receive additional deposit invoices once the balance of the original deposit payment has been expended.

If you are no longer interested in receiving these records, please reply to this email letting us know.  Your request will be closed March 9, 2026 if we do not receive the balance due. You will not owe any money if you choose not to pay the deposit invoice and your request will be closed without further action.

Please remit a check or money order (we cannot accept credit or debit cards), in the amount of $121.07, with your docket number PRR-2023-2850, to:

Attn: PRR-2023-2850
P.O. Box 1489
Tallahassee, FL 32302
